free_text: Delbruck and nuclear raman scattering of 10.83 MeV
photons by deformed and spherical heavy nuclei.
The absolute cross section of U at 90 degrees
was determined using procedure described earlier.
other absolute cross sections were obtained
by comparing the observed relative intensities
of scattered g-quanta with the uraniun value
Corrections for solid angle, detector efficiency,
the small spatial variation in beam intensity,
and absorption of photons were made. corrections
were made for the effect of absorption of the
incident and scattered photons in the target
material itself and for the absorption of
scattered photons in the detector germanium and
A beam of monoenergetic g-rays, extracted from the
CP-2 reactor, has been used in a high resolution
measurement of the differential cross section for
photon scattering by Tb, Ta, Pb, Bi, Th, and U-238.
for Pb and U extensive measurements of the angular
distributions for elastic and inelastic scattering
